---
format:
  revealjs:
    title: "Week 9 Lecture"
    subtitle: "Dr Shunya Kimura <br> skimura@liverpool.ac.uk"
    slide-number: true
    slide-level: 3
    output-file: "w9-slides-lecture.html"
    theme: ../scss/custom.scss
    revealjs-plugins:
      - pointer
    echo: true
---

### Sports Analytics in Practice {.not-bold}

#### Interactive Dashboards with Shinylive I

![](/images/UoL%20Zoom%20Backgrounds_20226.jpg){.stretch fig-align="center"}

### Module Outline

```{=html}
<div style="width:100%; font-family:Poppins,sans-serif; font-size:0.98em; margin-top:2em;">

    <div style="display:flex; padding:0.35em 0; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; color:#555;">
      <span style="width:3.2em; font-weight:600; color:#aaa;">W7</span>
      <span>Foundations of Reproducible Analytics</span>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; padding:0.35em 0; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; color:#555;">
      <span style="width:3.2em; font-weight:600; color:#aaa;">W8</span>
      <span>API &amp; Data Sourcing</span>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; padding:0.35em 0; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; color:#222;">
      <span style="width:3.2em; font-weight:700; color:#EF426F;">W9</span>
      <span style="font-weight:600;">Interactive Dashboards with Shinylive I</span>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; padding:0.35em 0; border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; color:#555;">
      <span style="width:3.2em; font-weight:600; color:#aaa;">W10</span>
      <span>Interactive Dashboards with Shinylive II</span>
    </div>
    <div style="display:flex; padding:0.35em 0; color:#555;">
      <span style="width:3.2em; font-weight:600; color:#aaa;">W11</span>
      <span>The Professional Portfolio</span>
    </div>

</div>
```

### Last Week

-   Used `hoopR` to pull NBA team and player data.
-   Inspected raw data frames before summarising.
-   Built cleaned team-level and player-level tables.
-   Saved raw pulls in `data/raw/` and cleaned files in `data/cleaned/`.

## Quick Recap

### Go To Workbook...

![](/images/workbook-shinyI.png){style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);"}

::: notes
Quickly recap. Then organise the file structure + .gitignore.
:::

### Our Plan

-   Understand what R Shiny is.
-   Learn the `ui` / `server` structure.
-   Connect user inputs to chart outputs.
-   Build a first local Shiny app from Workbook 3.

## What Is R Shiny?

### The Short Answer

-   Shiny is an R framework for building interactive web apps.
-   Users control inputs such as dropdowns, buttons and sliders.
-   R responds by updating charts, tables or text.

![](/images/rshiny-intro.png){style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);" fig-align="center"}

### Why Analysts Use It

-   Users can explore data without editing code.
-   One app can replace many repeated charts.
-   You can share analysis as a tool, not just a report.

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](/images/shiny-example-1.png){style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); width:95%;"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](/images/shiny-example-2.png){style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); width:95%;"}
:::
:::::

### The Mental Model

``` text
user changes input
  -> server code reacts
  -> output updates
```

The user does not see the R code. They see the app.

### Static Chart vs Shiny App

``` text
Static chart:
  "Here is Rui Hachimura."

Shiny app:
  "Choose any player, and the chart updates."
```

## Anatomy of a Shiny App

### One File: `app/app.R`

A basic Shiny app has four parts:

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| filename: "app/app.R"

library(shiny)

# 1. Load data

# 2. Define UI

# 3. Define server

# 4. Launch app
shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
```

### The Two Core Objects

``` text
ui
  what the user sees

server
  what R computes
```

Keep that split clear.

## UI

### What the UI Does

The UI defines the page.

It contains:

-   inputs, such as `selectInput()`;
-   output placeholders, such as `plotOutput()`;
-   layout, such as `fluidPage()` or `sidebarLayout()`.

### Minimal UI

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| filename: "app/app.R"

ui <- fluidPage(
  plotOutput("zone_bars")
)
```

This says: put a plot on the page and call it `zone_bars`.

### UI with a Dropdown

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| filename: "app/app.R"

player_choices <- sort(unique(shot_zones$player_name))

ui <- fluidPage(
  selectInput(
    inputId = "player",
    label = "Player",
    choices = player_choices,
    selected = "Rui Hachimura"
  ),
  plotOutput("zone_bars")
)
```

The dropdown value is stored as `input$player`.

### UI with Layout

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| filename: "app/app.R"

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Lakers Evolution Dashboard"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      selectInput("player", "Choose a player", player_choices)
    ),
    mainPanel(
      plotOutput("zone_bars", height = "500px")
    )
  )
)
```

Inputs usually go in the sidebar. Outputs usually go in the main panel.

## Server

### What the Server Does

The server fills the UI placeholders.

``` text
UI:
  plotOutput("zone_bars")

Server:
  output$zone_bars <- renderPlot(...)
```

The names must match.

### Minimal Server

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| filename: "app/app.R"

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$zone_bars <- renderPlot({
    shot_zones |>
      filter(player_name == "Rui Hachimura") |>
      ggplot(aes(x = shot_zone_basic, y = share, fill = era)) +
      geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7)
  })
}
```

This proves the plot can live inside Shiny.

### Reactive Server

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| filename: "app/app.R"

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  output$zone_bars <- renderPlot({
    shot_zones |>
      filter(player_name == input$player) |>
      ggplot(aes(x = shot_zone_basic, y = share, fill = era)) +
      geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7)
  })
}
```

Now the chart changes when the dropdown changes.

## Reactivity

### The Key Idea

You do not write:

``` text
when the dropdown changes, redraw the chart
```

Shiny handles that.

You write code that uses `input$player`, and Shiny watches it.

### Dependency

``` text
selectInput("player", ...)
        |
        v
input$player
        |
        v
renderPlot({ ... })
        |
        v
plotOutput("zone_bars")
```

If `input$player` changes, `renderPlot()` reruns.

### Names Must Match

``` text
selectInput("player", ...)
input$player

plotOutput("zone_bars")
output$zone_bars <- renderPlot(...)
```

Most beginner Shiny bugs are name mismatches.

## Build It in Stages

### Stage 1: Minimal App

-   Load `shot_zones.rds`.
-   Show one chart.
-   Hard-code one player.

Goal: prove the app runs.

### Stage 2: Add an Input

-   Create `player_choices`.
-   Add `selectInput()`.
-   Replace `"Rui Hachimura"` with `input$player`.

Goal: make the chart reactive.

### Stage 3: Add Layout

-   Add `titlePanel()`.
-   Use `sidebarLayout()`.
-   Put controls in `sidebarPanel()`.
-   Put the chart in `mainPanel()`.

Goal: make the app easier to use.

### Stage 4: Optional Plotly Output

The same UI/server pairing applies to Plotly.

``` text
plotOutput()    -> renderPlot()
plotlyOutput()  -> renderPlotly()
```

The Shiny idea is the same: output placeholder in the UI, render function in the server.

## Running the App

### Start the App

From the project root:

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| filename: "R code"

shiny::runApp("app")
```

This opens the local app in the RStudio viewer or browser.

### Good Development Habit

Build one layer at a time.

``` text
minimal app
  -> dropdown
  -> reactive chart
  -> layout
  -> optional Plotly
```

## Practical

### Go To Workbook...

![](/images/workbook-shinyI.png){style="box-shadow: 5px 5px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);"}

### Questions?

```{=html}
<div style="position:relative; height:460px;">
  <div style="position:absolute; top:58%; left:50%; transform:translate(-50%, -50%); text-align:center; width:70%;">
    <p style="font-size:1.2em; margin-bottom:0.5em;"><strong>Shunya Kimura</strong></p>
    <p style="font-size:1em; margin-bottom:0;">s.kimura@liverpool.ac.uk</p>
  </div>
  <div style="position:absolute; right:0; bottom:-42px; line-height:0;">
    <img src="../assets/ULMS744-logo.svg" alt="ULMS744 logo" style="width:220px; max-width:100%;">
  </div>
</div>
```
